title: malus pumila f. mitis
intro: Malus pumila f. mitis, also known as the wild apple or crabapple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the rose family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues, sore throats, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Malus pumila f. mitis include reducing fever, relieving headaches, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
